fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue expansion but currently unprofitable on a net basis and carrying significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 12.1%; next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY indicates strong market demand.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target of $358.82.
negatives: Profit margin -2.9% and operating margin 0.0% indicate unprofitability.
Return on equity -2.5% and return on assets -2.4% are negative.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.13 is relatively high.
Forward P/E of 43.40 suggests high growth expectations are priced in.

news.paragraph: MongoDB (MDB) is seeing continued analyst optimism, with recent reports highlighting maintained buy ratings and raised price targets from firms like Rosenblatt and Cantor Fitzgerald. The company's valuation is being supported by AI momentum and a significant one-year rally, though its growth metrics show some deceleration. With the next earnings date approaching, analysts are looking beyond just revenue and EPS to assess the company's valuation.
